description_long: This object is a stylized pink lotus flower with multiple layers of pointed petals radiating outward from a central core. The petals are arranged in a radial pattern, with the outermost layer being the largest and flattest, gradually decreasing in size and becoming more upright toward the center. The coloration transitions from a deeper pink on the outer petals to a lighter, almost cream color at the innermost petals, giving a natural gradient effect. The flower sits low and wide, with no visible stem or leaves, and the petals have a slightly angular, geometric appearance. The surface of the petals appears smooth and matte, with subtle shading that suggests a paper or lightweight plastic material. This flower would be suitable as a decorative element in urban parks, water features, or garden installations.
description: A stylized pink lotus flower with layered, pointed petals arranged radially and a soft color gradient from pink to cream. The petals are smooth, angular, and the flower sits low without a visible stem.
description_view_0: The flower is seen from a slightly elevated angle, highlighting the layered arrangement and the pointed, outward-reaching petals.
description_view_1: This view emphasizes the symmetry and radial spread of the petals, with the central core and inner petal layering clearly visible.
description_view_2: The flower appears flatter from this angle, showing the wide spread of the outer petals and the gentle gradient of color toward the center.
description_view_3: This view showcases the overlapping of petals and the geometric, angular shapes, with the innermost petals standing more upright.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, whorled configuration with multiple overlapping layers. Each layer of petals is offset from the previous, creating a symmetrical, circular pattern typical of lotus flowers.
